summary:
The test for HBV DNA will confirm active viral replication and guide further management during the suspected window period after initial exposure or acute phase resolution.
The absence of anti-HBc suggests either early stage infection not yet producing detectable antibodies against core antigen or post-infection recovery; thus, evaluating current viral load via HBV DNA testing becomes crucial before deciding treatment steps based purely off present immunoassay results alone. To ensure accurate diagnosis, checking both hepatitis B surface antibody levels alongside retesting within two months can provide clearer insights about immune response status towards potential reinfections too.

To ensure accurate diagnosis, checking both hepatitis B surface antibody levels alongside retesting within two months can provide clearer insights about immune response status towards potential reinfections too.. Answer choice: B
